title: Impact of AI Search Summaries on Website Traffic: Evidence from Google AI Overviews and Wikipedia
rationale: Directly measures AI Overviews' causal impact on Wikipedia traffic — a core empirical pillar for the traffic-decline claim.

title: The Impact of Generative AI on Collaborative Open-Source Software Development: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
rationale: Empirical evidence on how GitHub Copilot reshapes collaborative OSS contribution patterns — directly relevant to contributor pipeline erosion.

title: Wikipedia's Governance Challenge: Policies and Guardrails for New Generative AI Technologies
rationale: Examines Wikipedia's policy responses to GenAI — central to policy-response and synthetic-content contamination themes.

title: Self-Admitted GenAI Usage in Open-Source Software
rationale: Quantifies disclosed GenAI use in OSS commits, evidence of synthetic content entering the open-source knowledge commons.

title: The Disruption of Search Engine Optimization by Large Language Models: A Mixed-Methods Analysis of the Evolving Search Landscape
rationale: Mixed-methods analysis of LLM-driven shifts in search behaviour, relevant to information-seeking and traffic decline.

title: "Can You Tell Me?": Designing Copilots to Support Human Judgement in Online Information Seeking
rationale: Studies whether AI copilots can be designed to preserve human judgement during information seeking — direct counter-evidence for the literacy-erosion claim.

title: AI-powered Chatbot for New Wikipedia Editors
rationale: Demonstrates AI as a tool to support Wikipedia editor onboarding — counter-evidence that AI can bolster rather than erode the contributor pipeline.

title: Analyzing Wikipedia Edits: Patterns, Trends, and Insights from Collaborative Knowledge Construction
rationale: 2026 analysis of Wikipedia edit patterns may reveal recent contributor-pipeline trends; relevance hinges on whether AI effects are addressed.

title: Device-dependent click-through rate estimation in Google organic search results based on clicks and impressions data
rationale: CTR baseline for Google organic results pre-AI-Overviews; useful comparator but not AI-focused.

title: The Roles Bots Play in Wikipedia
rationale: Pre-LLM bot taxonomy provides background context for automated contributions but predates generative AI.

title: Not Good Enough, but Try Again! The Impact of Improved Rejection Communications on Contributor Retention and Performance in Open Knowledge Collaboration
rationale: Contributor retention mechanics in open knowledge platforms but no explicit AI angle.

title: An empirical examination of newcomer contribution costs in established OSS communities: a knowledge-based perspective
rationale: Background on OSS newcomer barriers; useful framing for pipeline erosion arguments but no AI dimension.

title: The Impact of AI-Powered Text Generation Tools on the Critical Thinking Skills of Undergraduate Students
rationale: Critical-thinking impact study among undergraduates — adjacent to literacy erosion but not tied to knowledge platforms.

title: The Impact of AI on Critical Thinking and Problem-Solving on Student Skills in Higher Education: An Empirical Study
rationale: Higher-education critical-thinking study; tangential to information-literacy theme without source-checking focus.

title: The influence of digital literacy and ChatGPT use on the critical thinking ability among library and information science students
rationale: LIS-student sample may touch on information literacy and source awareness, but it's an education-focused study.

title: Bots vs. Wikipedians, Anons vs. Logged-Ins (Redux): A Global Study of Edit Activity on Wikipedia and Wikidata
rationale: 2014 pre-LLM bot/edit-activity study with no bearing on generative-AI effects.

title: Private Peer Feedback as Engagement Driver in Humanitarian Mapping
rationale: Humanitarian-mapping engagement study unrelated to AI or general open-knowledge pipeline dynamics.

title: Why do newcomers abandon open source software projects?
rationale: 2013 OSS newcomer study predates the AI-effects timeframe.

title: Effects of Generative AI-Assisted Media Literacy Instruction on Learning Achievement and Cognitive Load among Elementary School Students
rationale: Classroom instructional study for elementary students with no link to open knowledge ecosystems.

title: Critical Thinking in the Age of Generative AI: Effects of a Short-Term Experiential Learning Intervention on EFL Learners
rationale: EFL classroom intervention; off-topic for knowledge-platform effects.

title: Enhanced Learning with Artificial Intelligence: A Comparative Study in Medical Students
rationale: Medical-student learning outcomes study unrelated to knowledge-platform ecosystems.
